profile.name: Sophia Alemar
profile.title: Graphic designer · Maryland-born · 19
profile.bio: I'm Sophia Alemar — I'm 19 and from Maryland. Art has been part of my life for as long as I can remember; even when I was little and did not have the vocabulary for it yet, I loved exploring new ways to express myself. I applied to study graphic design almost on a whim, and it turned out to be a genuine fit. It is a space where I can be both creative and professional, keep my hobbies close, and still protect my energy so I do not burn out.

projects.title: Webzine — Project 1
projects.tagline: Video preview on the grid; full interactive piece on GitHub Pages.
projects.description: This is my webzine: an interactive editorial layout you can explore in the browser. The tile on my portfolio plays a short preview clip; open the modal to watch it larger with controls, or use the button below to jump straight into the full experience.

projects.title: Postcards — DSGN 271
projects.tagline: Print set + a short fish animation from the same project.
projects.description: This project pairs a full postcard series with a small motion piece built from one of the illustrations. The grid tile loops the fish animation so you get a quick sense of the movement; open the project to watch it with sound and controls, then scroll to the flat view to see how the printed cards read together as a set.

projects.title: Hot sauce labels — Whimpy's & Velvet
projects.tagline: Two bottle concepts: playful patterning and a moody, editorial treatment.
projects.description: I designed the labels for both of these bottles. Whimpy's leans into a soft, handcrafted feel—creamy yellow grounds, lavender type, and a scattered pattern of tiny peppers and bottles. Velvet goes the opposite direction: a dark matte label, tall condensed lettering, and photography with wax, candles, and deep burgundy tones so the pack reads more like a small-batch ritual than a grocery shelf staple.

projects.title: You have to unlock your potential yourself.
projects.tagline: Mixed-media clock inspired by Stephanie Garber — Once Upon a Broken Heart.
projects.description: This piece is a hand-painted, interactive clock built around a hinged door: you open it to reveal a small painted scene—an apple, a reaching hand, floating papers, and branches against a blue-and-white void—with peach numbers and tiny white sparkles on a matte black face. It is inspired by Stephanie Garber and her path through more than two hundred rejections before the books she wanted to write found their audience. The idea is that progress stays interactive: you keep opening the next door yourself, on your own timeline, for the life you want to build.

projects.title: Custom typefaces — Word
projects.description: For this assignment I developed my own typefaces and documented the process in a print-ready layout: early sketches, full character sets, and samples in headlines and body copy so the personality of each face reads clearly. The PDF is the final deliverable from class.

projects.title: Hollywood × games — digital collage
projects.description: A digital collage that mixes a black-and-white Hollywood hillside with oversized Zelda props—the Hylian shield and sheathed Master Sword—over a lime field with a light grid, plus a tiny 8-bit Mario perched on the sign. The PDF collects the full class write-up and layout for this piece from my port folder.

projects.title: Port project — Final (alternate PDF)
projects.tagline: Second finalized export from the same brief.
projects.description: This is the companion PDF from the same port assignment (final 1-1). Open it to compare pagination, copy, or layout against the shorter final-1 export—whichever file matches what your instructor asked for is the one to lead with.

projects.title: Finall & 9 patterns
projects.tagline: Two PDFs from the same port project, shown together.
projects.description: These two files are one submission: Finall carries the main wrap-up, and 9 patterns documents the pattern exploration that supports it. In the modal they stack in that order so you can read straight through, with separate links if you prefer to open either PDF in its own tab.

projects.title: Banana bread — family cookbook spread
projects.tagline: Recipe layout celebrating my mom’s banana bread (PDF).
projects.description: I designed this cookbook-style spread around my mother’s banana bread: type, hierarchy, and pacing built for someone actually cooking at home—not a generic template. The PDF is what I turned in for class.

projects.title: Magazine spread — 10 items I would use
projects.description: For this assignment I designed a magazine-style spread around ten objects I would actually use: pacing, hierarchy, and image treatment so it reads like a real editorial feature rather than a flat list. The PDF is the final magazine file from class.
